title: The One-Week Overflow Check
subtitle: A daily noticing activity for building evidence that overflow is already present
excerpt: A seven-day activity for building a personal record of overflow as it already exists. Each day, write one specific moment, object, or experience that is more than you strictly need. By day seven the list shows a pattern: overflow is not a future destination but a current, observable fact.
sourceNotes: Adapted from the Day 63 activity theme of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program to Attract Cash, Overflow & Abundance Without Hustle or Burnout (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798268793796).

practice.whenToUse: Use when the idea of overflow feels abstract or like something that hasn't arrived yet, to build a concrete record of where it already exists in daily life.

content.text: For seven consecutive days, write one specific moment of overflow that appeared in the day. Not 'I am grateful for my house' — something concrete and dated. 'There was more bread than we needed and I gave the end of the loaf to a neighbour.' 'I had fifteen minutes extra before the call and spent it reading.' 'The account was higher than I expected when I checked it.'
The definition of overflow for this activity: anything that is more than strictly required. Time, food, money, kindness received, unexpected ease. The category is wide on purpose.
Each evening, spend two minutes before bed writing one line. Just one. The moment, what it was, what was extra about it.
Use a notebook, a notes app, or a single sheet of paper — whichever you will actually open every night.
Do not skip a day. A day with no overflow moment is useful data — write 'I noticed nothing today' and look at that closely.
On day seven, read all seven entries. Do not edit. Just notice what kind of overflow appears most often.
The record at the end of seven days is evidence — specific, dated, your own. It is a harder thing to argue against than an abstract affirmation.
Adapted from the Day 63 activity theme of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
